original: Super Mario Bros game first appears, created byShigeru Miyamotoat Nintendo
"Scooby-Doo Where are You" byHanna-Barberadebuts on CBS in the US
Swiss inventor George de Mestral is granted a patent for what would become known as Velcro
Paul Lynch of Great Britain does 32,573 push-ups in 24 hours
Michelangelobegins work on his statue of David, a masterpiece of Renaissance sculpture
USSR's Luna 2 becomes 1st probe to contact another celestial body
Space probe Ulysses passes the south pole of the Sun
English chemistMichael Faradaydiscovers the "Faraday effect," the influence of a magnetic field on polarized light
2nd Test flight of Space Shuttle Enterprise
Gilbert is measured the strongest (26.13 barometer) hurricane ever in the Western Hemisphere
British RAF pilot George Stainworth flies world speed record (655 kph)
Kim Zmeskal is 1st American to win a medal at World Gymnastics Championships, she wins the gold with 39.848 pts
17-year-old Cleveland Indians future Baseball HOF pitcherBob Fellerstrikes out then record 17 Philadelphia A's in 5-2 win at League Park, Cleveland
Walter Reed, U.S. Army physician and bacteriologist who proved mosquitoes transmit yellow fever, born in Belroi, Virginia (d. 1902)
America Lewis Howard Latimer invents and patents electric lamp with a carbon filament
ArchbishopDesmond Tutuleads biggest anti-apartheid protest march in South Africa
Béla Károlyi, Hungarian Romanian-American gymnastics coach (Nadia Comăneci, Mary Lou Retton, Kerri Strug), born in Kolozsvár, Hungary (d. 2024)[1]
Alleged world record temperature of 136.4°F (58°C) in El Aziziyah, Libya, in the shade (invalidated 2012 by the World Meteorological Organization)
Beatles win their 1st Grammy, for Best Group of 1964
1st airplane flight in Europe, Brazilian Alberto Santos-Dumont test flies his hybrid 14-bis plane (attached to a hydrogen balloon for take-off), at Bagatelle, France
Unmanned Mercury-Atlas 4 launched into Earth orbit
1st US University for African Americans, Xavier University, opens in New Orleans
Construction begins onHadrian's Wall in Northern England
MLB Oakland A's outfielderRickey Henderson3rd straight 100 steals for season
Don Bluth, American animator and film director (The Secret of NIMH), born in El Paso, Texas
Bong Joon-ho, South Korean film director (Parasite), born in Daegu, South Korea
Zak Starkey, English rock drummer (Oasis; The Who), son of Beatle Ringo Starr, born in Hammersmith, London
Michael Johnson, American 200m/400m runner (Olympic-2 gold-96), born in Dallas, Texas
Bernie Williams, Puerto Rican baseball outfielder (World Series 1996, 98–2000; ALCS MVP 1996; 5 × MLB All-Star; NY Yankees), born in San Juan, Puerto Rico
Richard Kiel, American actor (Jaws in "Moonraker"; Skidoo; So Fine), born in Detroit, Michigan, (d. 2014)
14-year-oldPriscillaBeaulieu meets 24-year-oldElvis Presleyat a party at his home in Bad Nauheim, Germany
US mint strikes 1st gold coin in 50 years (Olympic Eagle)
San Felipe Segundo/Okeechobee hurricane makes landfall in Puerto Rico as a category 5 storm, killing over 300, leaving 500,000 homeless, and causing $50M in damages (about $790M in 2020 dollars)
Oliver Evans, American inventor who pioneered high-pressure steam engine, born in Newport, Delaware (d. 1819)
Noël Godin, Belgian writer and humorist known for throwing a pie at Bill Gates, born in Liège, Belgium
Beatles release single "Yesterday" - just Paul, with strings arranged byGeorge Martin
MLB Brooklyn Robins pitcher Dazzy Vance no-hits the visiting Philadelphia Phillies, 10-1 at Ebbets Field
Pitcher Bob Trice becomes the first black player for Philadelphia Athletics
Chicago Cubs infielders Joe Tinker, Johnny Evers, &Frank Chanceappear together as teammates for 1st time, creating the famed double-play combination of "Tinker to Evers to Chance"

Italian Ferrari driverAlberto Ascariretains Formula 1 World Drivers Championship although forced to retire (engine trouble) in season ending Italian Grand Prix at Monza; wins title by 6.5 points fromJuan Manuel Fangio
T-Bone Walkerrecords his biggest hit "Call it Stormy Monday (But Tuesday Is Just as Bad)" in Hollywood, California
2nd Enterprise, approach & lands Test Cricket (ALT) flight (5m28s)
KGTV TV channel 10 in San Diego, CA (ABC/NBC) begins broadcasting
WPVI TV channel 6 in Philadelphia, PA (ABC) begins broadcasting
Ladies Pro Golf Association of America formed in NYC
1st United Negro College Fund
Albania leaves Warsaw pact
British beat French forces at the battle of Plains of Abrahams near Quebec - kills both leading generals Wolfe and Montcalm
Buckingham Palace damaged by German bombs
Lusitania arrives in New York City after record 5 day crossing of Atlantic
Rockslide near Whirlpool Rapids Bridge ends the Great Gorge and International Railway
Dike around Dutch polder Eastern Flevoland closes
Stravinsky's "Canticum Sacrum," premieres in Venice
Mother Teresa's State Funeral held in India
Elizabeth McCombs becomes the first New Zealand female Member of Parliament
Margaret Chase Smith, American politician (R-Me) elected senator, 1st woman to serve in both houses of US Congress
The main-belt asteroid "12 Victoria" is discovered by J R Hind
Predro Álvares Cabral and his Portuguese expedition arrive in Calicut, India
Portuguese seize trading ship Rahimi and its 700 passengers, the largest Mughal trading ship, belonging to the Empress Mariam-uz-Zamani, leads to a check on Portuguese power
General Belisarius of the Byzantine Empire defeats Gelimer and the Vandals at the Battle of Ad Decimium, near Carthage, North Africa
